item.title: ‘Japanese psycho-thriller’ game Wasabi announced for PC
item.content:encoded:
• PC

From Tokyo Underground Killer developer Phoenix Game Productions.

Tokyo Underground Killer developer Phoenix Game Productions has announced Wasabi, a “Japanese psycho-thriller” game set in 1990s Azumino City, Nagano. It will launch be available for PC via Steam. A release date was not announced.

Here is an overview of the game, via its Steam page:

About

Every day, a new corpse turns up in this quiet town. Every day, a new suspect emerges. And every day, that cave looms larger, more ominously. Amidst gory crime scenes and suffocating interrogations, only one thing will lead you to the truth: wasabi.

Ritualistic Murders in 90s Japan

The peace of Azumino is shattered by a series of bizarre murders. As Ryuhei Kuroshima, uncover the truth behind the nightmare.

Extract Confessions From Suspects

Eight suspects, each hiding secrets and motives of their own. Draw the truth from their lips with the power of wasabi.

Investigate Cryptic Crime Scenes

Each crime scene is more disturbing than the last. Follow every clue to uncover the truth behind the ritual murders.

Key Features

  • Intense Japanese psycho-thriller!
  • Set in a rural Nagano town in 1990s Japan.
  • Motion-captured interrogation scenes.
  • Interrogate eight suspects across six episodes.
  • Multiple endings.
  • Over 10 hours playtime.
  • Over 45 minutes of original music.

Watch the announcement trailer below.

Announce Trailer
